mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above the trailing 12-month average signals continued labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 142071k in July 2015, trending upward through mid-2015 expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-growth months do not outperform negative-growth months at p>0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound while other macro data strengthen, market anticipation of an imminent rate liftoff (first hike since 2006) triggers short-term equity de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as repricing of discount rates occurs.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS held below 0.25% for 6+ consecutive months while UNRATE falls below 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.13% in July 2015, near zero since 2008
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10) or Rank IC < 0.02 across rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of a falling unemployment rate approaching the Fed's implicit full-employment threshold and a federal funds rate still at the zero lower bound generates market expectations of accelerated policy tightening, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days that a single-series signal from either variable alone would not capture.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.3% and FEDFUNDS at 0.13% in mid-2015 approaching liftoff conditions
regime_hint: labor market tightening near zero-rate policy floor
failure_condition: Joint trigger periods show SPX 20-day forward returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.10), or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over univariate UNRATE/FEDFUNDS models.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in industrial production growth during a late-cycle expansion phase signals fading manufacturing momentum and impending margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days).
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.477 in June 2015, growth rate slowing relative to 2014
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: During periods of INDPRO deceleration in late-cycle regimes, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth expectations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 446477 million dollars in July 2015, trending upward
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month growth and forward 20d SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above a threshold that historically precedes construction-sector employment gains and durable goods spending signals broadening economic strength, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as growth expectations are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 1,100 thousand units on a 3-month moving average basis
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1174 thousand units in June 2015, above the post-crisis average
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST threshold breaches are not significantly positive (p > 0.10) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth relative to the prior 6-month average signals an abrupt weakening of labor demand that leads to immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as growth expectations are quickly repriced.
candidate_trigger: monthly PAYEMS change falls more than 50% below the trailing 6-month average change
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS growth has been steady near 200k/month through mid-2015, providing a baseline for deceleration detection
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following PAYEMS deceleration triggers are not significantly negative (p > 0.10) across the historical sample, or trigger fires fewer than 5 times in any 5-year period preventing testability.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime of persistently low inflation (CPIAUCSL growth below target) combined with near-zero federal funds rate, the market prices in a prolonged period of accommodative policy, supporting a sustained positive equity risk premium and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; this effect requires the joint condition of low inflation and low rates rather than either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.786 in June 2015 with subdued YoY inflation, FEDFUNDS at 0.13% still near zero
failure_condition: During periods meeting both low-inflation and low-rate conditions, SPX 60-day forward returns underperform unconditional baseline (p < 0.10), or the interaction effect is not statistically significant relative to univariate CPIAUCSL or FEDFUNDS models.
